story.text: As the loop, I need the live surface bdo judges on to stop changing without him. Today `.github/workflows/pages.yml` deploys main's HEAD to bdf1992.github.io/ontum on every push, with no owner stamp — the one promotion that escapes him (epic.environments glue). This is the first buildable slice of his confirmed arc: the production gate, smallest real teeth. The spine is non-circular by deploying the STAMPED snapshot, never HEAD — so the act of recording a stamp can never become unstamped live content. A `production_promotion` admission (by: bdo, a snapshot commit sha, supersedable) is his stamp, the same authority as confirm-arc: the gate executes his gesture, it never invents a verdict (the .github/ law, the arc's second guardrail). Before his first stamp the gate is in loud unstamped passthrough so landing it never dark-takes the live site — the teeth engage on his first promotion, the way atom-invariant engages on his branch-protection gesture.

briefing.headline: the production gate: the live URL only moves on bdo's stamp
briefing.value: loop/deploy.py — a stdlib, read-only fold over the committed log that names the current production snapshot (the latest non-superseded bdo-authored production_promotion) and a gate verdict for a candidate commit (promote iff it is the stamped snapshot, else hold serving the stamped one), plus the one pen verb `promote` that refuses every non-bdo signer. .github/workflows/pages.yml now serves the snapshot the gate names instead of unconditionally serving HEAD.
briefing.why_now: epic.environments is confirmed and landed (PR #277). The production gate is its wave-1 first buildable slice (the epic names it `atom.production-gate.v0`, 'smallest real teeth') and closes the unstamped-live gap directly — the recurring failure the arc was authorized to fix.
briefing.if_accepted: bdo's stamp reaches the one promotion it never reached: the live surface he judges on stops changing without him. The promotion record is the deployment-ledger groundwork the staging-rung piece (wave 2) builds on.
briefing.if_rejected: the live URL keeps auto-deploying every push with no owner stamp — the gap the arc exists to close stays open.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and fail-safe: the fold is read-only and deterministic; the only writer is bdo's stamp through one pen; before the first stamp the gate is loud passthrough, so landing it cannot take the live site down. Reverting is deleting loop/deploy.py and the workflow steps.
briefing.mechanism: loop/deploy.py: production_promotion / production_snapshot / gate / promote, CLI for read-only status, the `gate --candidate` CI seam (exit 0 promote/passthrough, non-zero hold), and the bdo-only `promote` stamp. pages.yml gains a gate step that names the deploy target and a checkout-the-stamped-snapshot step. tests/test_deploy.py joins the suite with §10 teeth: a stamped snapshot promotes and is served, an un-stamped candidate is HELD (the two locally-fine records refuse to fit), a superseding promotion moves the live snapshot, a non-bdo promote is refused at the pen, a forged non-bdo promotion is ignored by the fold, and a constant gate cannot pass both the held and promoted case. Done-line 0137.
